executive_summary.feasibility_rationale: Calculated timeline of ~38 weeks (expected) fits within 12-month constraint with buffer for contingency.
top_risks: Cross-service dependency complexity during parallel modernization of 3 services on DevOps pathway
No Kubernetes experience — team requires EKS training before containers work stream
Plaintext secrets in 2 repositories require immediate remediation before any migration begins

work_streams.name: Move to Modern DevOps
work_streams.objective: Establish CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code (CDK), automated testing, and progressive deployment strategies for all 3 affected services, enabling safe and repeatable deployments.

tasks.description: Design and provision shared CI/CD platform using AWS CDK — CodePipeline, CodeBuild projects, artifact repositories (ECR, CodeArtifact)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: CDK stack deploys successfully; CodePipeline skeleton operational for at least one service

tasks.description: Establish IaC patterns and CDK construct library for shared infrastructure components (VPC, security groups, IAM roles)
tasks.acceptance_criteria: CDK construct library published; VPC and baseline security groups deployable via CDK

tasks.description: Implement secrets management migration — move plaintext credentials to AWS Secrets Manager for local-monolith and unishop-monolith
tasks.acceptance_criteria: No plaintext secrets in source code; all credentials retrieved from Secrets Manager at runtime

tasks.description: Implement CI/CD pipeline for local-monolith — build, test, deploy stages with automated quality gates
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Pipeline triggers on push; runs lint, unit tests, builds container, deploys to staging

tasks.description: Implement CI/CD pipeline for unishop-monolith — build, test, deploy stages with IaC provisioning via CDK
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Pipeline deploys infrastructure and application; IaC coverage > 80%

tasks.description: Implement CI/CD pipeline for aws-microservices — build, test, deploy stages with CDK synth and diff
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Pipeline triggers on push; CDK deploy runs with approval gate for production

tasks.description: Add integration test suites for local-monolith and aws-microservices — minimum 70% coverage on critical paths
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Integration tests pass in CI; critical API paths covered; test results published to pipeline

tasks.description: Implement progressive deployment strategy (blue/green) for local-monolith using App Runner traffic splitting
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Blue/green deployment operational; rollback completes in < 5 minutes

tasks.description: Implement progressive deployment strategy for aws-microservices using Lambda aliases and weighted routing
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Canary deployment with automatic rollback on error rate > 1%

success_criteria: All 3 services have operational CI/CD pipelines with automated quality gates
IaC coverage > 80% for unishop-monolith infrastructure
Integration test coverage on critical paths for all 3 services
Progressive deployment (blue/green or canary) operational for at least 2 services
Zero plaintext secrets in any repository

work_streams.name: Move to Containers
work_streams.objective: Containerize the unishop-monolith Java application and deploy on Amazon EKS with Graviton-based node groups, enabling horizontal scaling and preparing for cloud-native decomposition.

tasks.description: Create multi-stage Dockerfile for unishop-monolith Java application — optimize for Graviton (ARM64) with distroless base image
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Docker image builds successfully for ARM64; image size < 300MB; application starts and passes health checks

tasks.description: Provision EKS cluster with Graviton-based managed node groups using CDK — include Karpenter for autoscaling
tasks.acceptance_criteria: EKS cluster operational; Graviton nodes running; Karpenter scales pods within 60 seconds

tasks.description: Create Kubernetes manifests (Deployment, Service, Ingress, HPA) and Helm chart for unishop-monolith
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Application deploys to EKS via Helm; health checks pass; HPA scales based on CPU/memory

tasks.description: Configure network policies, pod security standards, and service mesh (optional) for production hardening
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Network policies enforce least-privilege; pod security admission enforced; no privileged containers

tasks.description: Integrate EKS deployment into CI/CD pipeline — kubectl apply via CodeBuild with image scanning
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Pipeline deploys to EKS on merge to main; ECR image scanning blocks deployment on critical CVEs

tasks.description: Implement multi-AZ deployment with pod disruption budgets and rolling update strategy
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Pods distributed across 3 AZs; PDB prevents simultaneous pod termination; zero-downtime updates verified

success_criteria: unishop-monolith running on EKS with Graviton nodes
Horizontal pod autoscaling operational
Multi-AZ deployment with 99.95% availability target
CI/CD pipeline deploys to EKS with image scanning
Network policies and pod security standards enforced

work_streams.name: Move to Managed Databases
work_streams.objective: Migrate unishop-monolith from self-managed MySQL to Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL with Multi-AZ, automated backups, and encryption at rest using customer-managed KMS keys.

tasks.description: Assess database schema, stored procedures, and MySQL-specific features — create migration compatibility report for Aurora PostgreSQL
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Compatibility report complete; all incompatible SQL identified; migration approach documented

tasks.description: Provision Aurora PostgreSQL cluster using CDK — Multi-AZ, encryption with CMK, PITR enabled, version pinned
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Aurora cluster operational; Multi-AZ verified; PITR enabled; CMK encryption confirmed

tasks.description: Execute schema migration from MySQL to Aurora PostgreSQL — use AWS DMS for initial data load and ongoing replication
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Schema migrated; data replicated with < 1 second lag; validation queries pass

tasks.description: Update application connection strings and data access layer — adapt SQL queries for PostgreSQL dialect
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Application connects to Aurora PostgreSQL; all CRUD operations functional; integration tests pass

tasks.description: Perform cutover — redirect traffic to Aurora PostgreSQL, validate data consistency, decommission self-managed MySQL
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Zero data loss verified; application latency within baseline; rollback plan tested

success_criteria: unishop-monolith running on Aurora PostgreSQL (Multi-AZ)
Customer-managed KMS encryption at rest enabled
Point-in-time recovery (PITR) enabled with 35-day retention
Database version pinned with automated minor version upgrades
Zero data loss during migration

work_streams.name: Move to Cloud Native
work_streams.objective: Decompose local-monolith and unishop-monolith into microservices with event-driven communication patterns, deploying on EKS with service mesh for inter-service communication.

tasks.description: Conduct domain-driven design workshop — identify bounded contexts, define service boundaries for both monoliths
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Bounded context map documented; service boundaries defined; data ownership assigned per context

tasks.description: Design event-driven architecture — define event schemas, choose EventBridge as event bus, establish event catalog
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Event catalog published; EventBridge bus provisioned; event schema registry populated

tasks.description: Establish shared microservices template — CDK constructs for EKS service, database, event publishing, health checks
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Template microservice deploys to EKS; publishes events to EventBridge; health endpoint operational

tasks.description: Extract Order service from unishop-monolith — implement strangler-fig pattern with API Gateway routing
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Order API served by new microservice; monolith no longer handles order operations; integration tests pass

tasks.description: Extract Inventory/Catalog service from unishop-monolith — separate database per service pattern
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Catalog service operational with own database; event-driven sync with Order service verified

tasks.description: Implement async communication patterns for unishop — event-driven order processing via EventBridge and SQS
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Order events published to EventBridge; downstream services consume asynchronously; no synchronous coupling

tasks.description: Extract Product/Catalog service from local-monolith PHP application — rewrite in Java/Spring Boot for EKS deployment
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Catalog API served by new Java microservice on EKS; feature parity with PHP implementation verified

tasks.description: Extract Order Management service from local-monolith — implement with async order processing via SQS
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Order service handles all order operations; long-running processes handled asynchronously

tasks.description: Extract User/Authentication service — integrate with Amazon Cognito for centralized identity
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Authentication via Cognito; JWT token-based auth across all microservices; session migration complete

tasks.description: Implement end-to-end integration tests across decomposed services — verify event flows and API contracts
tasks.acceptance_criteria: End-to-end test suite passes; event delivery confirmed; API contract tests green

tasks.description: Decommission monolith remnants — remove unused code paths, update API Gateway routing to 100% microservices
tasks.acceptance_criteria: All traffic served by microservices; monolith process stopped; no regression in error rates

success_criteria: Both monoliths decomposed into independently deployable microservices
Event-driven communication via EventBridge between services
Each microservice has its own database (database-per-service pattern)
Async processing for long-running operations via SQS
End-to-end integration tests pass across all decomposed services
Zero synchronous coupling between bounded contexts

milestones.name: First Service Decomposed
gate_criteria: At least one bounded context extracted

parallelization_strategy.description: Independent work streams run in parallel; dependent work streams run sequentially. WS-03 (Managed Databases) runs in parallel with WS-01 (DevOps) since they have no dependency relationship.

parallel_groups.rationale: WS-03 has no prerequisites and is not dependent on WS-01. Both can execute concurrently.
WS-02 depends on WS-01 completion. Runs after Group 1.
WS-04 depends on WS-01 and WS-02. Runs last.

risk_register.description: Cross-service dependency complexity creates integration failures during parallel modernization of 3 services on the DevOps pathway
risk_register.mitigation: Establish shared CI/CD patterns and CDK construct library before per-service implementation; use feature flags for incremental rollout
risk_register.contingency: Serialize per-service DevOps work streams if integration issues arise; extend timeline by 4 weeks
risk_register.trigger: More than 2 failed deployments in a single week across different services

risk_register.description: Team of 8 spread across 4 pathways reduces velocity per work stream — risk of context switching overhead
risk_register.mitigation: Assign dedicated sub-teams to each active work stream (e.g., 4 on DevOps, 2 on DB migration during Phase 1); minimize concurrent active streams to 2
risk_register.contingency: Reduce parallelism to single active work stream; extend timeline
risk_register.trigger: Sprint velocity drops below 60% of baseline for 2 consecutive sprints

risk_register.description: Critical path of 34 weeks (expected) leaves 18 weeks of buffer within 12-month constraint, but pessimistic estimate of 52 weeks consumes entire timeline
risk_register.mitigation: Prioritize critical path work streams; de-scope WS-03 if behind schedule (can complete post-migration); weekly timeline tracking
risk_register.contingency: Defer WS-04 Phase 4 (local-monolith decomposition) to follow-on engagement if timeline pressure mounts
risk_register.trigger: Actual completion > 120% of expected duration for any work stream on critical path

risk_register.description: SOC2 and PCI-DSS compliance must be maintained during migration transition periods — dual-running systems increase audit surface
risk_register.mitigation: Maintain compliance controls in both old and new systems during transition; engage compliance team in architecture reviews; implement audit logging from day 1
risk_register.contingency: Extend dual-running period to allow full compliance validation before cutover; engage external auditor for gap assessment
risk_register.trigger: Compliance audit finding or gap identified during migration

risk_register.description: No Kubernetes experience on team — EKS adoption (WS-02, WS-04) requires significant upskilling before productive work
risk_register.mitigation: Schedule EKS training (AWS EKS Workshop) in first 4 weeks concurrent with WS-01; pair with AWS Solutions Architect for initial cluster setup
risk_register.contingency: Engage AWS Professional Services or partner for EKS implementation; team focuses on application-level tasks
risk_register.trigger: Team unable to independently troubleshoot EKS issues after training period

risk_register.description: Budget constraint of $1.2M may be tight if pessimistic timeline materializes or if additional infrastructure costs exceed estimates
risk_register.mitigation: Use Graviton instances for 20% cost savings; leverage Savings Plans; track infrastructure spend weekly; implement cost allocation tags from day 1
risk_register.contingency: Reduce scope — defer WS-04 local-monolith decomposition; optimize team utilization by reducing to 6 FTEs
risk_register.trigger: Monthly burn rate exceeds $120K for 2 consecutive months

risk_register.description: MySQL to Aurora PostgreSQL migration (WS-03) may encounter incompatible SQL patterns or application-level issues
risk_register.mitigation: Run AWS SCT (Schema Conversion Tool) early in assessment phase; identify all incompatibilities before migration; maintain rollback capability
risk_register.contingency: Fallback to Aurora MySQL (compatible engine) instead of PostgreSQL if conversion complexity is too high
risk_register.trigger: SCT reports > 20% of SQL statements require manual conversion

people.assumptions: 8 engineers at $250/hr, 70% allocation to modernization, calendar duration varies by estimate

infrastructure.assumptions: Delta infrastructure cost during migration — includes EKS cluster, Aurora PostgreSQL, dual-running during transition. $3,000-5,000/month for 24-36 weeks.

training.assumptions: EKS training ($15K), CDK/IaC training ($15K), Cloud Native patterns ($15K), Aurora PostgreSQL ($15K). Team has Java/Spring — no training needed there.

leading_indicators.target: >= 80% of planned story points per sprint

exit_criteria: All 4 triggered pathways fully addressed — no High-severity MODA gaps remaining
Portfolio MODA re-score improvement: average score increase from 2.31 to >= 3.0
All services in scope achieve Pilot-Ready or Cloud-Native Ready classification
SOC2 and PCI-DSS compliance maintained throughout — zero audit findings
99.95% availability maintained during and after migration

assumptions: Team of 8 engineers available at 70% capacity for modernization work (30% on BAU/support)
No major production incidents requiring sustained attention during migration period
MODA reports reflect current state (assessed 2026-05-18, within 90-day currency window)
Infrastructure and tooling budget approved up to $1.2M
AWS account access and IAM permissions pre-provisioned
EKS training can be scheduled within first 4 weeks of engagement
Database migration window of 4 hours available for final cutover (low-traffic period)
SOC2 and PCI-DSS audit cycles known and planned around
Customer decision points will be resolved within 1 week of being raised

decision_points.question: What CI/CD platform should be used for the portfolio — AWS-native (CodePipeline/CodeBuild) or GitHub Actions?

options.pros: Tight AWS integration, IAM-native auth, no external dependencies
options.cons: Less community ecosystem, steeper learning curve for complex workflows

options.pros: Team may have existing familiarity, large marketplace, flexible workflows
options.cons: External dependency, requires OIDC federation setup, egress costs

decision_points.recommendation: AWS CodePipeline + CodeBuild — aligns with CDK preference, eliminates external dependency, and integrates natively with EKS deployment targets

decision_points.question: Should unishop-monolith database migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL (preferred) or Aurora MySQL (compatible)?

options.pros: Better JSON support, richer extension ecosystem, preferred per steering context, Graviton-optimized
options.cons: Requires SQL dialect conversion, higher migration risk

options.pros: Drop-in compatible with existing MySQL, minimal application changes, lower risk
options.cons: Does not align with aurora-postgresql preference, smaller extension ecosystem

decision_points.recommendation: Aurora PostgreSQL — aligns with stated preference and provides better long-term foundation. Use AWS SCT to assess conversion complexity before committing.

decision_points.question: What decomposition approach should be used for the monolith-to-microservices migration?

options.pros: Lower risk, services extracted one at a time, rollback possible per service, maintains availability
options.cons: Longer duration, dual-running complexity, requires API Gateway routing

options.pros: Clean architecture from start, no legacy code maintained
options.cons: High risk, long period without production validation, no incremental value delivery

decision_points.recommendation: Strangler-fig pattern — required for 99.95% availability SLA and aligns with moderate risk tolerance. Extract highest-value bounded contexts first.

decision_points.question: Should the team invest in formal EKS training or learn on-the-job with AWS Professional Services guidance?

options.pros: Structured learning, hands-on labs, certification path, builds long-term self-sufficiency
options.cons: 2-week calendar impact on WS-01 timeline, training cost

options.pros: Immediate productivity, learn in context of actual workload, no separate training window
options.cons: Higher consulting cost, knowledge transfer risk, dependency on external expert

decision_points.recommendation: Formal training with parallel WS-01 execution — team splits: 4 on DevOps tasks, 4 in EKS training during weeks 1-2. Provides self-sufficiency by WS-02 start.

decision_points.question: Should local-monolith be rewritten in Java/Spring (team strength) or modernized in PHP 8+ for initial containerization?

options.pros: Aligns with team expertise, better cloud-native tooling, consistent stack across portfolio
options.cons: Higher effort, risk of feature gaps, longer timeline for WS-04

options.pros: Faster initial containerization, preserves existing logic, lower risk of bugs
options.cons: PHP cloud-native ecosystem is smaller, team less experienced in modern PHP, may need rewrite later anyway

decision_points.recommendation: Rewrite in Java/Spring Boot during decomposition — leverages team's strongest capability and creates a consistent portfolio stack. Strangler-fig approach means services are extracted one at a time, managing risk.
